item.title: How many upvotes is Robert Picardo worth to you people?
item.dc:creator: Fuzzynubz

item.title: Agency
item.description: Despite women gaining the right to vote over 100 years ago, financial freedom is a much more recent development. You read the right – it wasn't until 1974 and the passage of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act that women were finally able to apply for a credit card or a loan on their own, without a male co-signer. Prior to 1974 banks required married women to have their spouse's signature on credit applications and could deny credit cards to single women. The law made it illegal for a financial institution to discriminate against applicants based on gender, race or religion.
item.dc:creator: Magemenes

item.title: It is time for another unhinged XL Tuesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
don't think i've ever done that
just relax.. let it stop moving
Felix has them trained well
it's free real estate
finally
it's getting bad
still like it occasionally
mmm hmm
i can toucnh it
Maine is unhinged
uh oh
why
brilliant
eh not that bad
donut man is terrifyingly delicious
fuck yeah
that’s my time, have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Exhausted
item.description: Every day I wake up and it seems like the price of everything has skyrocketed, and we're just supposed to bend over and take it. Ten years ago, a 4 1/2 apartment went for $735, now it's $2,200. McD's used to have a value menu with $0.89 cheeseburgers and now they're charging $3.15 for the same shitty item. Frozen pizzas: $10, butter: $7, eggs: $8. I feel so powerless, so frustrated.
item.dc:creator: IrateOldMan

item.title: Feeling powerless with a hint of rage.

And no other horizons than this.

item.title: Physicist Peter Higgs passed away today
item.description: With several other physicists, in 1964 he proposed the existence of a field that gives mass to all matter in the universe. The particle associated with this field, the Higgs boson, was observed by the Large Hadron Collider in 2012.
item.dc:creator: BuffaloKnight

item.title: They are discussing who is gonna take care of the baby lol

item.title: Chosen one!
item.dc:creator: DCA4242

item.title: Just
item.description: A woman’s struggles are historically just… not that serious. They’re brushed off, dismissed, or flat out ignored because they’re “just” not that big of a deal. Eliminating this way of thinking is crucial. It can lead to earlier diagnoses, proper treatments, and healthier, fuller lives. In a survey of women between the ages of 18-64: 29% say their doctor had dismissed a health concern they brought up 25% say a healthcare provider didn’t believe they were telling the truth 19% say a doctor assumed something about them without asking them first 13% say a healthcare provider has suggested that they’re to blame for a health issue. Additionally, the NHS was a good idea, initially, but today it's the embodiment of chaos and a petri dish for malpractice, especially when it comes to women patients. Better communication and attention to bias and discrimination will start to knock down the barriers that women face when trying to receive quality, necessary care. This is a PSA for doctors to hear us, see us, and advocate for us through these challenges—not minimize them and keep it moving.
item.dc:creator: Magemenes

item.title: Fuck this hits hard, good to know I'm not alone, and if you've through it, going through it, you're definitely not alone either!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.description: In 1954 Dorothy Dandridge became the first Black woman nominated for an #Oscar for best actress for her role in “Carmen Jones.” Fast forward nearly 50 years… Halle Berry became the first Black woman to actually win the award for her work in “Monster’s Ball.” We can’t help but think she would’ve made Dorothy proud, for not only portraying her beautifully in a biopic, but making history and earning a “first” for Black actresses everywhere.
item.dc:creator: Magemenes

item.title: Solar Eclipse 2024
item.description:
This composite image shows the progression of a partial solar eclipse over the Washington Monument. Credit: NASA/Bill Ingalls⁣
Solar prominences by Trevor Mahlmann
Montréal by Anaïs Remili.
The entire sequence as seen from Presque Isle, Maine by John Kraus.
Cleveland by Gabe Wasylko.
item.dc:creator: Nofakepotatoes

item.title: Dad advice. It’s been a really tough week to be a dad as one (adult) kid has broken up with their long time SO. We are all sad. Here’s some “fatherly advice and how to’s” from my stash I send to my sons. Upvote and comment with your own if you like.
item.dc:creator: Wheretheheckisjason
